VENLAFAXINE||TRAMADOL.clinical_note: Risk of serotonin syndrome and seizures. Use alternative analgesic.

TRAMADOL||VENLAFAXINE.subject: TRAMADOL
TRAMADOL||VENLAFAXINE.mechanism: Both have serotonergic activity; increased serotonin syndrome risk
TRAMADOL||VENLAFAXINE.clinical_note: Risk of serotonin syndrome and seizures. Use alternative analgesic.

DULOXETINE||TRAMADOL.subject: DULOXETINE
DULOXETINE||TRAMADOL.mechanism: Both serotonergic; duloxetine also inhibits CYP2D6 reducing tramadol efficacy
DULOXETINE||TRAMADOL.clinical_note: Dual risk: serotonin syndrome and reduced analgesia. Avoid combination.

TRAMADOL||DULOXETINE.subject: TRAMADOL
TRAMADOL||DULOXETINE.mechanism: Both serotonergic; duloxetine also inhibits CYP2D6 reducing tramadol efficacy
TRAMADOL||DULOXETINE.clinical_note: Dual risk: serotonin syndrome and reduced analgesia. Avoid combination.

TRAZODONE||FLUOXETINE.subject: TRAZODONE
TRAZODONE||FLUOXETINE.mechanism: Fluoxetine inhibits CYP3A4/2D6, increasing trazodone levels
TRAZODONE||FLUOXETINE.clinical_note: Increased sedation and risk of serotonin syndrome. Start trazodone at low dose.

FLUOXETINE||TRAZODONE.subject: FLUOXETINE
FLUOXETINE||TRAZODONE.mechanism: Fluoxetine inhibits CYP3A4/2D6, increasing trazodone levels
FLUOXETINE||TRAZODONE.clinical_note: Increased sedation and risk of serotonin syndrome. Start trazodone at low dose.

QUETIAPINE||KETOCONAZOLE.subject: QUETIAPINE
QUETIAPINE||KETOCONAZOLE.mechanism: Ketoconazole inhibits CYP3A4, increasing quetiapine exposure 6-fold
QUETIAPINE||KETOCONAZOLE.clinical_note: Reduce quetiapine dose to 1/6th when used with strong CYP3A4 inhibitors.

KETOCONAZOLE||QUETIAPINE.subject: KETOCONAZOLE
KETOCONAZOLE||QUETIAPINE.mechanism: Ketoconazole inhibits CYP3A4, increasing quetiapine exposure 6-fold
KETOCONAZOLE||QUETIAPINE.clinical_note: Reduce quetiapine dose to 1/6th when used with strong CYP3A4 inhibitors.

COLCHICINE||CLARITHROMYCIN.subject: COLCHICINE
COLCHICINE||CLARITHROMYCIN.mechanism: Clarithromycin inhibits CYP3A4 and P-glycoprotein; colchicine has narrow therapeutic index
COLCHICINE||CLARITHROMYCIN.clinical_note: Fatal colchicine toxicity reported. Reduce colchicine dose significantly or avoid combination.

CLARITHROMYCIN||COLCHICINE.subject: CLARITHROMYCIN
CLARITHROMYCIN||COLCHICINE.mechanism: Clarithromycin inhibits CYP3A4 and P-glycoprotein; colchicine has narrow therapeutic index
CLARITHROMYCIN||COLCHICINE.clinical_note: Fatal colchicine toxicity reported. Reduce colchicine dose significantly or avoid combination.

DABIGATRAN||AMIODARONE.subject: DABIGATRAN
DABIGATRAN||AMIODARONE.mechanism: Amiodarone inhibits P-glycoprotein, increasing dabigatran levels by 12-60%
DABIGATRAN||AMIODARONE.clinical_note: Reduce dabigatran dose to 150mg daily in patients with renal impairment.

AMIODARONE||DABIGATRAN.subject: AMIODARONE
AMIODARONE||DABIGATRAN.mechanism: Amiodarone inhibits P-glycoprotein, increasing dabigatran levels by 12-60%
AMIODARONE||DABIGATRAN.clinical_note: Reduce dabigatran dose to 150mg daily in patients with renal impairment.

APIXABAN||KETOCONAZOLE.subject: APIXABAN
APIXABAN||KETOCONAZOLE.mechanism: Dual CYP3A4 and P-glycoprotein inhibition doubles apixaban exposure
APIXABAN||KETOCONAZOLE.clinical_note: Reduce apixaban dose by 50% when combined with strong CYP3A4 + P-gp inhibitors.

KETOCONAZOLE||APIXABAN.subject: KETOCONAZOLE
KETOCONAZOLE||APIXABAN.mechanism: Dual CYP3A4 and P-glycoprotein inhibition doubles apixaban exposure
KETOCONAZOLE||APIXABAN.clinical_note: Reduce apixaban dose by 50% when combined with strong CYP3A4 + P-gp inhibitors.

RIVAROXABAN||RIFAMPIN.subject: RIVAROXABAN
RIVAROXABAN||RIFAMPIN.mechanism: Rifampin induces CYP3A4 and P-glycoprotein, reducing rivaroxaban efficacy by 50%
RIVAROXABAN||RIFAMPIN.clinical_note: Avoid combination. Reduced anticoagulant effect increases thrombotic risk.

RIFAMPIN||RIVAROXABAN.subject: RIFAMPIN
RIFAMPIN||RIVAROXABAN.mechanism: Rifampin induces CYP3A4 and P-glycoprotein, reducing rivaroxaban efficacy by 50%
RIFAMPIN||RIVAROXABAN.clinical_note: Avoid combination. Reduced anticoagulant effect increases thrombotic risk.

METFORMIN||RENAL_FAILURE.subject: METFORMIN
METFORMIN||RENAL_FAILURE.mechanism: Impaired renal clearance of metformin leads to accumulation
METFORMIN||RENAL_FAILURE.clinical_note: Contraindicated if eGFR <30. Reduce dose if eGFR 30-45. Risk of fatal lactic acidosis.

METFORMIN||LIVER_DISEASE.subject: METFORMIN
METFORMIN||LIVER_DISEASE.clinical_note: Avoid in patients with clinical or laboratory evidence of hepatic disease. Risk of lactic acidosis.

ACE_INHIBITORS||PREGNANCY.subject: ACE_INHIBITORS
ACE_INHIBITORS||PREGNANCY.mechanism: ACE inhibitors are teratogenic; cause fetal renal dysgenesis, oligohydramnios
ACE_INHIBITORS||PREGNANCY.clinical_note: CONTRAINDICATED in all trimesters. Discontinue immediately upon pregnancy detection.

ACE_INHIBITORS||ANGIOEDEMA.subject: ACE_INHIBITORS
ACE_INHIBITORS||ANGIOEDEMA.mechanism: ACE inhibitor-induced angioedema can be life-threatening
ACE_INHIBITORS||ANGIOEDEMA.clinical_note: History of angioedema with any ACE inhibitor is absolute contraindication for all ACE inhibitors.

ACE_INHIBITORS||BILATERAL_RENAL_ARTERY_S.subject: ACE_INHIBITORS
ACE_INHIBITORS||BILATERAL_RENAL_ARTERY_S.mechanism: ACE inhibitors remove efferent arteriolar tone, causing acute renal failure
ACE_INHIBITORS||BILATERAL_RENAL_ARTERY_S.clinical_note: Can cause acute anuria. Also applies to unilateral stenosis of a solitary kidney.

WARFARIN||ACTIVE_BLEEDING.subject: WARFARIN
WARFARIN||ACTIVE_BLEEDING.clinical_note: Absolute contraindication. Reverse anticoagulation with vitamin K, FFP, or PCC.

NSAIDS||PEPTIC_ULCER.subject: NSAIDS
NSAIDS||PEPTIC_ULCER.mechanism: NSAIDs inhibit protective prostaglandins in gastric mucosa
NSAIDS||PEPTIC_ULCER.clinical_note: High risk of GI hemorrhage. Use acetaminophen or add PPI gastroprotection.

NSAIDS||CHRONIC_KIDNEY_DISEASE.subject: NSAIDS
NSAIDS||CHRONIC_KIDNEY_DISEASE.mechanism: NSAIDs reduce renal blood flow by inhibiting prostaglandin-mediated afferent arteriolar vasodilation
NSAIDS||CHRONIC_KIDNEY_DISEASE.clinical_note: Can precipitate acute kidney injury. Avoid in CKD stage 3 or worse.

NSAIDS||HEART_FAILURE.subject: NSAIDS
NSAIDS||HEART_FAILURE.mechanism: NSAIDs cause sodium and water retention, worsening heart failure
NSAIDS||HEART_FAILURE.clinical_note: Increased risk of decompensation and hospitalization.

BETA_BLOCKERS||SEVERE_ASTHMA.subject: BETA_BLOCKERS
BETA_BLOCKERS||SEVERE_ASTHMA.clinical_note: Non-selective beta-blockers absolutely contraindicated. Cardioselective agents (bisoprolol) may be used cautiously in mild asthma.

BETA_BLOCKERS||SEVERE_BRADYCARDIA.subject: BETA_BLOCKERS
BETA_BLOCKERS||SEVERE_BRADYCARDIA.mechanism: Further depression of AV node conduction and heart rate
BETA_BLOCKERS||SEVERE_BRADYCARDIA.clinical_note: Contraindicated if heart rate <50 bpm or second/third degree heart block.

STATINS||ACTIVE_LIVER_DISEASE.subject: STATINS
STATINS||ACTIVE_LIVER_DISEASE.mechanism: Statins undergo hepatic metabolism and can worsen liver injury
STATINS||ACTIVE_LIVER_DISEASE.clinical_note: Contraindicated if transaminases persistently >3x ULN. Check LFTs before starting.

STATINS||PREGNANCY.subject: STATINS
STATINS||PREGNANCY.mechanism: Cholesterol is essential for fetal development; statins may be teratogenic
STATINS||PREGNANCY.clinical_note: Contraindicated in pregnancy and breastfeeding. Discontinue 1 month before planned conception.

CIPROFLOXACIN||MYASTHENIA_GRAVIS.subject: CIPROFLOXACIN
CIPROFLOXACIN||MYASTHENIA_GRAVIS.clinical_note: Risk of respiratory failure. Avoid all fluoroquinolones in myasthenia gravis patients.

CIPROFLOXACIN||QT_PROLONGATION.subject: CIPROFLOXACIN
CIPROFLOXACIN||QT_PROLONGATION.clinical_note: Avoid in patients with congenital or acquired long QT syndrome. Monitor ECG if unavoidable.

LITHIUM||RENAL_FAILURE.subject: LITHIUM
LITHIUM||RENAL_FAILURE.mechanism: Lithium relies entirely on renal elimination
LITHIUM||RENAL_FAILURE.clinical_note: Contraindicated in severe renal impairment. Dose adjustment required in mild-moderate CKD.

LITHIUM||DEHYDRATION.subject: LITHIUM
LITHIUM||DEHYDRATION.mechanism: Volume depletion increases proximal tubular lithium reabsorption
LITHIUM||DEHYDRATION.clinical_note: Risk of lithium toxicity. Ensure adequate hydration. Hold lithium during acute illness with dehydration.

METHOTREXATE||PREGNANCY.subject: METHOTREXATE
METHOTREXATE||PREGNANCY.mechanism: Potent teratogen; inhibits folate metabolism essential for fetal development
METHOTREXATE||PREGNANCY.clinical_note: Category X. Wait at least 3 months (men) or 1 ovulatory cycle (women) after stopping before conception.

METHOTREXATE||LIVER_DISEASE.subject: METHOTREXATE
METHOTREXATE||LIVER_DISEASE.mechanism: Methotrexate is hepatotoxic and can cause fibrosis/cirrhosis
METHOTREXATE||LIVER_DISEASE.clinical_note: Avoid in alcoholic liver disease or chronic hepatitis. Monitor LFTs regularly.

METHOTREXATE||IMMUNODEFICIENCY.subject: METHOTREXATE
METHOTREXATE||IMMUNODEFICIENCY.clinical_note: Risk of fatal opportunistic infections. Contraindicated in existing immunodeficiency syndromes.

ASPIRIN||PEDIATRIC_VIRAL_ILLNESS.subject: ASPIRIN
ASPIRIN||PEDIATRIC_VIRAL_ILLNESS.mechanism: Aspirin in children with viral illness (influenza, varicella) causes Reye syndrome
ASPIRIN||PEDIATRIC_VIRAL_ILLNESS.clinical_note: CONTRAINDICATED in children <18 with fever or viral illness. Use acetaminophen or ibuprofen.

TETRACYCLINES||PREGNANCY.subject: TETRACYCLINES
TETRACYCLINES||PREGNANCY.mechanism: Permanent discoloration of developing teeth; inhibition of bone growth
TETRACYCLINES||PREGNANCY.clinical_note: Avoid in pregnancy and children under 8. Use amoxicillin or azithromycin as alternatives.

VALPROIC_ACID||PREGNANCY.subject: VALPROIC_ACID
VALPROIC_ACID||PREGNANCY.mechanism: Neural tube defects (spina bifida) in 1-2% of exposed pregnancies
VALPROIC_ACID||PREGNANCY.clinical_note: Most teratogenic antiepileptic. Use lamotrigine or levetiracetam in women of childbearing potential.

THIAZOLIDINEDIONES||HEART_FAILURE.subject: THIAZOLIDINEDIONES
THIAZOLIDINEDIONES||HEART_FAILURE.mechanism: TZDs cause fluid retention via renal sodium reabsorption
THIAZOLIDINEDIONES||HEART_FAILURE.clinical_note: Contraindicated in NYHA Class III-IV heart failure. Pioglitazone linked to edema and weight gain.

ERGOTAMINE||PERIPHERAL_VASCULAR_DISEASE.subject: ERGOTAMINE
ERGOTAMINE||PERIPHERAL_VASCULAR_DISEASE.clinical_note: Risk of gangrene. Also contraindicated in coronary artery disease and hypertension.

CLOZAPINE||AGRANULOCYTOSIS.subject: CLOZAPINE
CLOZAPINE||AGRANULOCYTOSIS.mechanism: Clozapine-induced agranulocytosis occurs in 1% of patients
CLOZAPINE||AGRANULOCYTOSIS.clinical_note: Requires mandatory ANC monitoring (REMS program). Absolute contraindication if prior clozapine-induced agranulocytosis.

SILDENAFIL||SEVERE_HYPOTENSION.subject: SILDENAFIL
SILDENAFIL||SEVERE_HYPOTENSION.mechanism: Further vasodilation can cause cardiovascular collapse
SILDENAFIL||SEVERE_HYPOTENSION.clinical_note: Contraindicated if systolic BP <90mmHg. Also avoid within 24 hours of nitrate use.

DIGOXIN||HYPOKALEMIA.subject: DIGOXIN
DIGOXIN||HYPOKALEMIA.mechanism: Hypokalemia sensitizes the myocardium to digoxin toxicity
DIGOXIN||HYPOKALEMIA.clinical_note: Correct potassium before starting digoxin. Maintain K+ >4.0 mEq/L during therapy.

CORTICOSTEROIDS||SYSTEMIC_FUNGAL_INFECTI.subject: CORTICOSTEROIDS
CORTICOSTEROIDS||SYSTEMIC_FUNGAL_INFECTI.clinical_note: Systemic corticosteroids contraindicated in active systemic fungal infections.

AMINOGLYCOSIDES||MYASTHENIA_GRAVIS.subject: AMINOGLYCOSIDES
AMINOGLYCOSIDES||MYASTHENIA_GRAVIS.clinical_note: Risk of respiratory failure. Use alternative antibiotics when possible.

OPIOIDS||RESPIRATORY_DEPRESSION.subject: OPIOIDS
OPIOIDS||RESPIRATORY_DEPRESSION.clinical_note: Absolute contraindication in severe respiratory depression. Have naloxone available.

ISOTRETINOIN||PREGNANCY.subject: ISOTRETINOIN
ISOTRETINOIN||PREGNANCY.mechanism: Extremely potent teratogen; causes craniofacial, cardiac, and CNS malformations
ISOTRETINOIN||PREGNANCY.clinical_note: iPLEDGE program mandatory. Two forms of contraception required. Negative pregnancy test monthly.

SULFONAMIDES||G6PD_DEFICIENCY.subject: SULFONAMIDES
SULFONAMIDES||G6PD_DEFICIENCY.mechanism: Oxidative stress from sulfonamides triggers hemolytic anemia in G6PD-deficient patients
SULFONAMIDES||G6PD_DEFICIENCY.clinical_note: Screen for G6PD deficiency before prescribing. Use alternative antibiotics.

CHLOROQUINE||RETINAL_DISEASE.subject: CHLOROQUINE
CHLOROQUINE||RETINAL_DISEASE.mechanism: Chloroquine accumulates in the retinal pigment epithelium causing irreversible maculopathy
CHLOROQUINE||RETINAL_DISEASE.clinical_note: Annual ophthalmologic screening recommended after 5 years of use. Stop if retinal changes detected.

CARBAMAZEPINE||BONE_MARROW_DEPRESSION.subject: CARBAMAZEPINE
CARBAMAZEPINE||BONE_MARROW_DEPRESSION.mechanism: Carbamazepine can cause aplastic anemia and agranulocytosis
CARBAMAZEPINE||BONE_MARROW_DEPRESSION.clinical_note: Obtain baseline CBC. Contraindicated in patients with prior bone marrow depression.

DOXYCYCLINE||PREGNANCY.subject: DOXYCYCLINE
DOXYCYCLINE||PREGNANCY.mechanism: Tetracyclines cause permanent tooth discoloration and inhibit fetal bone growth
DOXYCYCLINE||PREGNANCY.clinical_note: Avoid in pregnancy. Use amoxicillin or azithromycin when possible.

FLUCONAZOLE||QT_PROLONGATION.subject: FLUCONAZOLE
FLUCONAZOLE||QT_PROLONGATION.mechanism: Fluconazole can prolong QT interval, especially at high doses
FLUCONAZOLE||QT_PROLONGATION.clinical_note: Caution with doses >400mg/day. Avoid in patients with baseline QT prolongation.

TRAMADOL||SEIZURE_DISORDER.subject: TRAMADOL
TRAMADOL||SEIZURE_DISORDER.clinical_note: Risk of seizures, especially at higher doses or with SSRIs/TCAs. Use alternative analgesic.

SPIRONOLACTONE||HYPERKALEMIA.subject: SPIRONOLACTONE
SPIRONOLACTONE||HYPERKALEMIA.mechanism: Spironolactone blocks aldosterone, reducing potassium excretion
SPIRONOLACTONE||HYPERKALEMIA.clinical_note: Contraindicated if serum K+ >5.5 mEq/L. Monitor potassium within 1 week of starting.

ALLOPURINOL||ACUTE_GOUT.subject: ALLOPURINOL
ALLOPURINOL||ACUTE_GOUT.mechanism: Rapid urate lowering can paradoxically worsen acute gout flare
ALLOPURINOL||ACUTE_GOUT.clinical_note: Do not initiate during acute gout attack. Wait 2-4 weeks after flare resolution. Start at low dose with colchicine prophylaxis.
